tagline: A Rajajinagar landmark for Radha Krishna darshan, kirtan, prasadam, festivals, and visitor-friendly spiritual learning.
historical_significance: ISKCON Sri Radha Krishna Temple on Hare Krishna Hill is one of Bengaluru’s best-known devotional destinations. The official temple site highlights daily darshan, festivals, lectures, bhajans, prasadam distribution, volunteering, guest house support, and devotee services; this demo keeps those details curated into a simple visit-planning flow instead of copying the full website.

special_days.name: Panihati Chida-dahi Utsava
special_days.extended_hours: Festival updates are announced by the temple.

special_days.name: Sri Jagannatha Ratha Yatra
special_days.extended_hours: Festival schedule may vary across ISKCON Bengaluru centres.

dress_code: Formal or traditional attire is recommended; avoid shorts and casual clothing as a mark of respect.
special_entry_rules: Photography is not allowed inside temple interiors. Only small bags are allowed for security reasons. Parking and footwear stand may have nominal charges.

sevas.name: Offer Seva
sevas.description: A guided demo path for visitors who want to support deity worship or festival services.

sevas.name: Bhagavad-gita Learning Visit
sevas.description: A demo request flow inspired by the temple’s learning programs and lecture culture.

accommodation_details: Yatri Nivas / guest-house support is listed by the official temple site.
special_events_text: ISKCON Bengaluru hosts vibrant festivals throughout the year — from Ratha Yatra to Panihati Utsava — drawing thousands of devotees. The temple also conducts daily bhajans, lecture programs, and cultural activities open to all.

events.title: Panihati Chida-dahi Utsava
events.description: Official festival listing highlighted on ISKCON Bangalore’s site.

events.title: Sri Jagannatha Ratha Yatra
events.description: Official festival listing for the Bengaluru ISKCON community.

faqs.question: What are the official darshan timings used in this demo?
faqs.answer: Morning 4:15 AM to 5:15 AM and 7:15 AM to 1:15 PM, evening 4:15 PM to 8:00 PM, with no afternoon break on weekends and public holidays.

faqs.question: Is entry free?
faqs.answer: Yes. The official timings page lists temple entry as free, with nominal charges for parking and footwear stand.

faqs.question: Is prasadam available?
faqs.answer: The official FAQ says free lunch prasadam is served daily between 11:30 AM and 2:00 PM.

faqs.question: Is parking available?
faqs.answer: Yes. The official FAQ says parking is available for two-wheelers and four-wheelers with nominal charges.

faqs.question: What is the dress code?
faqs.answer: Formal or traditional attire is advised; avoid shorts and casual clothing.

faqs.question: Are wheelchair and lift facilities available?
faqs.answer: Yes. The official contact and FAQ pages mention elevator, wheelchair, and ramp support for elderly and specially-abled visitors.

visit_plan_title: Plan Your Visit to ISKCON Bengaluru

visit_highlights.label: Before You Go
visit_highlights.title: Choose a darshan window
visit_highlights.description: Weekdays are split into morning and evening darshan windows; weekends and public holidays continue through the afternoon.

visit_highlights.label: On Campus
visit_highlights.title: Use visitor facilities first
visit_highlights.description: Plan footwear, parking, small-bag security, wheelchair support, restrooms, and lift access before entering darshan areas.

visit_highlights.label: After Darshan
visit_highlights.title: Stay for prasadam or learning
visit_highlights.description: The official site highlights daily lunch prasadam, lectures, bhajans, volunteering, and festival participation.

visitor_advisories.title: Photography
visitor_advisories.description: Photography is not allowed inside the temple interiors.

visitor_advisories.title: Best Visit Window
visitor_advisories.description: Early morning around 7–9 AM or the evening arati period (after 4 PM) offer the most serene darshan experience.

visitor_advisories.title: Accessibility
visitor_advisories.description: Wheelchair, ramp, and elevator support are listed for elderly and specially-abled visitors.
